studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary and secondary outcomes were measured at baseline, post-treatment (3 months after randomization), and during follow-ups at 6 and 12 months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received access to the HelloBetter ratiopharm chronic pain digital therapy program, which consists of 7 units and aims to reduce pain interference. The control group received care-as-usual (CAU), which includes unrestricted access to routine care options such as general practitioners, pain physicians, psychotherapeutic treatment, drug therapy, physical therapy, and exercise therapy. After the 3-month data collection, control group participants could access the online program if desired.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Pain interference measured by the West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI) interference scale at 3 months. Secondary outcomes: Emotional functioning (PHQ-9), health-related quality of life (AQOL-8D), pain intensity and interference (BPI), multidimensional pain interference (MPI), pain impairment in everyday life (PDI), anxiety (GAD-7), pain processing (FESV), pain acceptance (CPAQ), psychological flexibility (FAH-II, PIPS), pain-specific self-efficacy (PSEQ), patient satisfaction (CSQ-8, intervention group only), negative effects (NEQ, intervention group only), and subjective global assessment of change (PGIC). Data were collected at baseline, post-treatment (3 months), and follow-ups at 6 and 12 months.

studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The study includes three visits per patient: baseline (V1), telephone visit after four weeks (V2), and a visit at month three (V3).
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Group 1 Intervention: Patients with axial spondyloarthritis (axSpA) who continue to suffer from pain despite adequate anti-inflammatory medication are prescribed the HelloBetter ACT-app. Group 2 Control: Patients with axSpA who continue to suffer from pain despite adequate anti-inflammatory medication receive standard therapy without prescription of the HelloBetter ACT-app.
studies.collected_data: Demographic characteristics, educational and employment status, current therapy (pharmacological and non-pharmacological), pain assessment (NRS: global pain, back pain (BASDAI question 2), nocturnal back pain, West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI-D), neuropathic pain using the painDetect questionnaire), assessment of morning stiffness (BASDAI questions 5 and 6), PASS patient acceptable symptom state, mental health (Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS)), disease activity (Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Score (ASDAS), Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Index (BASDAI)), physical functioning (Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Functional Index (BASFI)), spinal mobility (Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Metrology Index (BASMI)), physical activity (modified Short Questionnaire to Assess Health enhancing physical activity (mSQUASH)), global functioning (ASAS Health Index (ASAS HI)), health-related quality of life (Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire (FIQ), Short Form 36 (SF36)), patient and physician global assessment, Patient Activation Measure (PAM12), patient global impression of change, Mobile Application Rating Scale (MARS), questionnaire on system usability.

studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.intervention_and_control_group: I1 = guided, I2 = unguided, C = waitlist control
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1: Guided GET.ON chronic pain intervention with feedback emails from an e-coach after each lesson. Arm 2: Unguided GET.ON chronic pain intervention without feedback emails, participants work autonomously. Arm 3: Waitlist control group.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: Pain interference (MPI Interference-Scale) at pre-(T0), post-(T1, 9 weeks after randomisation), and follow-up (T2, 6 months after randomisation). Secondary Outcomes: At screening: demographic variables (sex, age, education, social support), prior pain treatments, type, chronicity, and comorbidities by self-report. At T0, T1, and T2: Brief Pain Inventory (BPI), Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9), Generalised Anxiety Disorder Screener (GAD-7), pain intensity (11-point numerical rating scale, NRS), quality of life (Short Form 12, SF-12; EuroQol, EQ-5D), Acceptance and Action Questionnaire-II (AAQ-II, German version: FAH-II), Chronic Pain Acceptance Questionnaire (German version: CPAQ-D), adapted Trimbos/iMTA questionnaire for costs associated with psychiatric illness (TiC-P). Treatment process (after lessons 2, 4, and 6): FAH-II and CPAQ-D. At T1 and T2: Patient Global Impression of Change scale (PGIC). Active groups only: intervention adherence based on attrition rate, satisfaction with intervention (Client Satisfaction Questionnaire, CSQ-8, German version: ZUF-8).

audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
